title: Add search/filter input to DS sidebar for finding structures quickly
description:
QA REVIEW GAP: 34 structures in sidebar with no search capability. User must scroll through 6 categories to find 'Bloom Filter'. Algorithm module has a dropdown selector — DS module has nothing.

Add a search input at top of DSSidebar (below 'Data Structures / 34 structures' header). Typing 'bloom' filters the list to show only Bloom Filter. Typing 'tree' shows all 14 tree structures. Empty search shows all.

This is how every IDE file browser works — instant search over a list.
acceptanceCriteria: Search input at top of DS sidebar
Typing filters structures by name in real-time
Search is case-insensitive
Empty search shows all 34 structures
Categories auto-collapse if they have zero matching items
Keyboard shortcut (Cmd+F or /) focuses the search input

title: Add bulk data import — paste array from clipboard or enter comma-separated values
description:
QA REVIEW GAP: Currently users must type values one at a time and click Insert repeatedly. No way to paste '[5, 3, 8, 1, 9, 2]' and build a BST with 6 nodes in one action.

Add a 'Paste Data' or 'Import' button next to Random that opens a small textarea. User pastes comma-separated values (e.g., '5, 3, 8, 1, 9, 2') and clicks 'Build'. The module inserts all values sequentially with step generation.

Alternatively: detect paste event in the value input and parse multi-value input automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can input multiple values at once (comma-separated)
Works for: Array (direct set), BST/AVL/RB (sequential insert), Heap (build), Hash (sequential insert)
Paste event in value input detected and parsed
Invalid values highlighted with error message
At least 100 values supported

title: Add animation speed slider to playback controls (0.25x to 4x)
description:
QA REVIEW GAP: DST-071 adds auto-play but no user-controlled speed. Students need to slow down for complex operations (AVL rotations) and speed up for simple ones (array traverse).

Add a speed slider or speed buttons (0.25x, 0.5x, 1x, 2x, 4x) next to the Step/Play controls. Speed multiplies the base animation duration (currently 0.3s). At 0.25x: 1.2s per step. At 4x: 0.075s per step.

Algorithm module comparison: the Algorithm module sidebar has a 'Random Size' slider (5-100). DS module has no equivalent control.
acceptanceCriteria: Speed control visible in playback controls area
Speed options: 0.25x, 0.5x, 1x, 2x, 4x
Speed affects both auto-play interval and animation transition duration
Current speed displayed visually
Speed persists within session (not across refresh)

title: Add copy-to-clipboard buttons for pseudocode, summaries, and complexity tables
description:
QA REVIEW GAP: DST-134 adds pseudocode panel and DST-145 adds 3-bullet summaries, but neither specifies a copy button. Students need to copy complexity info, pseudocode, and summaries to their notes/flashcards.

Add a small copy icon button (clipboard icon) to:
1. Pseudocode panel (copy entire pseudocode block)
2. Complexity table (copy as formatted text)
3. Summary bullets (copy as text)
4. Step descriptions (copy current step)

Use navigator.clipboard.writeText() with a brief 'Copied!' toast feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: Copy button on pseudocode panel copies pseudocode text
Copy button on complexity table copies formatted complexity info
Copy button on summary copies 3-bullet takeaway
Brief 'Copied!' toast feedback on click
Buttons are small/unobtrusive (icon only, no label)

title: Add export DS visualization as PDF cheat sheet
description:
QA REVIEW GAP: Zero print/PDF export capability. Students want a printable one-pager per DS showing: name, description, complexity table, visualization snapshot, key operations pseudocode, real-world applications.

Implement: 'Export PDF' button in properties panel. Uses browser print or html2canvas + jspdf to generate a clean one-page summary. Alternatively: a 'Print View' mode that reformats the current DS into a printer-friendly layout.

This enables: flashcard creation, study sheets, professor handouts.
acceptanceCriteria: Export/Print button accessible from properties panel
Generated PDF includes: name, description, complexity, visualization snapshot
PDF is clean and printer-friendly (no dark background)
Works for all 34 DS
File named '{ds-name}-cheatsheet.pdf'

title: Add i18n infrastructure for DS descriptions and step text
description:
QA REVIEW GAP: Non-English speaker persona identified in Phase 4 innovation but zero i18n tasks created. All descriptions and step text are hardcoded English strings.

Add i18n infrastructure:
1. Extract all DS_CATALOG descriptions into a locale file (en.json)
2. Create translation keys for step description templates
3. Add language selector (initially: English only, but infrastructure ready for Spanish, Chinese, Hindi)
4. Use next-intl or react-i18next for the translation layer

This is infrastructure — actual translations come later. But the architecture must support it.

Note: Priority P3 because this is infrastructure for future expansion, not an immediate user need.
acceptanceCriteria: DS descriptions loaded from locale file, not hardcoded
Step description templates use interpolation-safe format
Language selector UI exists (even if only English available)
Adding a new language requires only adding a JSON file
No hardcoded English strings in engine files
